findings.message: Both new 'allowed on current branch' tests are inert: they build fixtures but never invoke `execute_catalogue_spec_signals`, so the success path for the rewritten branch guard and catalogue-spec file writes is no longer exercised. A regression in the matching-branch path would still pass this suite.

`current_branch()` is folded into `unwrap_or_default()` here, so a git branch-read failure is treated the same as an ordinary non-track branch and rejected with the mismatch path. That contradicts the briefing’s fail-closed requirement for git discover/branch-read failures, and it can silently mask repo/git errors instead of surfacing them. The same pattern is repeated in the catalogue-spec and pre-commit call sites.

`track-commit-message` still fail-opens before the new branch guard: when `current_branch_track_id_strict()` returns `None` for `main`, a detached/non-track branch, or a git branch-read failure, this `if let` simply skips `run_pre_commit_type_signals` and continues to CI/commit. That means the required branch-based guard never rejects non-track branches for the make pre-commit path, contrary to the new fail-closed `track/<id>`-only policy.

The PR subcommand help still advertises `track/plan` branch support and `--track-id` for `plan/` branches, but `resolve_branch_context` now delegates to `resolve_pr_branch`, which rejects every non-`track/<id>` branch and ignores the explicit track ID. Users following this help on a removed `plan/<id>` lane will get a hard error instead of the documented push/ensure/review behavior, so the plan-lane expunge is left with a stale public CLI contract.

`usecase-types.json` cites `CN-07` as the branch-based guard in multiple docs entries (see lines 60, 178, 201, and 250), but this track's spec only defines `CN-01`..`CN-04`. That makes the reference unresolved and the contract notes internally inconsistent.

T003 gives two incompatible control-flow contracts for branch mismatch: it first requires `run_pre_commit_type_signals` to return `Err(CliError::Message(...))` instead of `None`, then says `type_signal_bindings_opt` `None` should mean guard failure while also removing the caller's `None` check. The plan needs one coherent fail-closed path; as written, implementers can either keep a silent skip or remove the only handling for the guard failure state.

T005 omits tests for the `make.rs` branch guard even though T003 adds branch validation to `run_pre_commit_type_signals` and AC-04 depends on the pre-commit path. The plan can satisfy T005 with tests for `signals.rs`, `catalogue_spec_signals.rs`, and `render.rs` while leaving the new pre-commit fail-closed behavior untested.

IN-01 explicitly covers removing the status guard from all artifact-writing actions including `sync-views`, but the coverage map lists only T001-T003. T004 is the task that changes `sync_rendered_views`, so the generated coverage can mark IN-01 complete without the sync-views change that IN-01 itself requires.

The usecase catalogue gives conflicting guidance for the new branch guard: `PreCommitTypeSignalsError` adds explicit `BranchNotFound` / `BranchMismatch` variants as the replacement for the old frozen guard, but the `PreCommitTypeSignalsInteractor` docs say branch mismatch is delegated to `TypeSignalsInteractor` and surfaced only as `TypeSignalsRecomputeFailed`. That leaves implementers with two incompatible error contracts for the same pre-commit branch mismatch path, so the plan should pick one propagation model and make the catalogue/impl-plan agree.

The Related note mislabels `knowledge/adr/2026-05-26-0518-active-track-write-guard.md` as a "render guard". That ADR is about replacing the frozen status gate with branch-based write validation, so the description is semantically wrong and can mislead readers about why the two ADRs are related.

S6 claims the T006 compile unit covers `IN-06 partial`, but `IN-06` is specifically the deletion of `.claude/commands/track/plan-only.md` and `.claude/commands/track/activate.md`, while T006 only covers CLI/usecase/Makefile code deletion and `task-coverage.json` maps `IN-06` solely to T007. This leaves the plan artifacts with conflicting requirement-to-task ownership; remove `IN-06 partial` from S6 or move the command-file deletion into T006.

`domain-types.json` names the removed phase as `ReadyToActivate`, but this scope's spec and implementation plan both use `TrackPhase::ActivateTrack` ([spec.json](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/track/items/active-track-write-guard-2026-05-26/spec.json#L145), [impl-plan.json](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/track/items/active-track-write-guard-2026-05-26/impl-plan.json#L48)). The same `ReadyToActivate` wording also appears in [usecase-types.json](/home/flip451/individual/t-rust/templates/SoTOHE-core/track/items/active-track-write-guard-2026-05-26/usecase-types.json#L210), so the generated catalogue is internally inconsistent about which enum arm is being deleted and could point implementers at the wrong variant.

`WorktreeGuardError` and the `usecase::worktree_guard::*` delete entries are traced to `IN-11`, but `IN-11` is specifically about removing `plan/<id>` branch handling from shared modules like `pr_workflow`, `merge_gate`, `task_completion`, and `track_resolution`. The entries themselves describe deleting the worktree cleanliness guard because it was part of the activate lane, which is a different requirement, so the catalogue now points these deletions at the wrong spec element.

P1: the fail-closed branch guard is only surfaced when `spec.json` exists. In a workspace that has protected type catalogues (`architecture-rules.json` + `<layer>-types.json`) but no spec, a git lookup failure is downgraded to `false` and the function returns `Ok`, silently skipping type rendering instead of returning `InvalidTrackMetadata`. That violates the briefing’s CN-01 requirement for types-only tracks.

P1: the new mismatched-branch test does not actually cover the types half of the guard. It never writes any `<layer>-types.json` and only asserts that `spec.md` is absent, so a regression that still renders `*-types.md` on the wrong branch would pass unnoticed.

The git-failure arm aborts the whole sync as soon as any `*-types.json` is present, so callers in a non-git workspace never reach the unconditional `contract-map.md` render. That regresses the existing done-track contract-map path and also suppresses the style-config error surfaced by `render_contract_map_view`; the fail-closed branch should be narrowed or deferred until after the unguarded render.

The new 'matching branch' test is a placeholder: it only reads the current branch and writes fixtures into a temp dir, but never calls `sync_rendered_views` or asserts any rendered output. The branch-match success path is therefore still untested.

The fail-closed branch error is only deferred when `architecture-rules.json` exists and a type catalogue is discovered. If git lookup fails on a track that has protected `*-types.json` inputs but no rules file, `sync_rendered_views` returns `Ok` after rendering `plan.md` and silently skips the protected views, which contradicts the new CN-01 fail-closed behavior.

`track_dir_contains_type_catalogue()` only treats entries with `DirEntry::file_type().is_file()` as catalogues. That misses symlinked `*-types.json` files, even though the render loop later accepts them via `catalogue_path.is_file()`. As a result, a symlinked protected catalogue can bypass the deferred branch error when git lookup fails and the sync will succeed without rendering the view.

`git` lookup failures are only converted into `InvalidTrackMetadata` when `spec.json` exists or `track_dir_contains_type_catalogue()` finds a readable `*-types.json` file. On a non-git checkout with only `plan.md`/`registry.md` inputs, `sync_rendered_views` now succeeds instead of failing closed, which contradicts the branch-guard requirement in the briefing.

Branch-read failures are only surfaced after `render_contract_map_view()`. If `architecture-rules.json` or the contract-map style config is missing/malformed, that error wins and the intended fail-closed `InvalidTrackMetadata` for git lookup failure is never returned. The brief says the branch error should be deferred only until after `plan.md`.

`sync_rendered_views(track_id)` now requires a git repo even for registry-only refreshes, but the unchanged tests at `sync_rendered_views_writes_plan_and_registry` and `sync_rendered_views_omits_unchanged_registry_from_changed_set` still use plain tempdirs without `init_git_repo_on_branch()`. They will now return `InvalidTrackMetadata` before their assertions, so the test suite regresses.

`git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` returns success with `HEAD` in detached-HEAD checkouts, so this branch guard treats a non-branch checkout as a normal mismatch (`Ok(false)`) instead of failing closed. That silently skips `spec.md`/`<layer>-types.md` on detached CI/worktree checkouts even when the active track should be rendered.

The branch guard now returns an InvalidTrackMetadata error immediately after plan.md whenever git branch lookup fails, even when there are no protected inputs to render. That makes plan-only or spec/types-absent calls fail instead of simply updating plan.md/registry.md, despite the guard being scoped to spec.md and <layer>-types.md; it also breaks existing tests such as the spec-absent/types-absent plan-only cases that call sync_rendered_views outside a git repo. Delay the fail-closed error until a spec.json or configured type catalogue actually needs the protected branch decision.

`has_type_catalogue_input` detects protected type inputs by scanning only filenames ending in `-types.json`, but the renderer’s actual input set comes from `architecture-rules.json` and `TdddLayerBinding::catalogue_file()`, which supports custom simple filenames such as `custom.json` and renders them to `custom.md`. With `spec.json` absent, git branch lookup failing, and a configured custom catalogue present, this check returns false and `sync_rendered_views` succeeds after `plan.md` while silently skipping the protected rendered type view instead of failing closed. That leaves supported custom catalogue files outside the new CN-01 branch guard.

Dropping the branchless-planning tie-breaker makes `render_registry` able to surface a newer branchless v5 planned track as `Latest active track` again, even though `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/latest_track.rs` still treats branchless planned v3/v4/v5 tracks as lower-priority placeholders. The registry and verifier now disagree on which active track should win.

The replacement test no longer verifies the `Current Focus` row or the recommended command for the branchless fixture; it only matches the generic `/track:status` footer, so it would still pass if the current-focus ordering regressed.

`selection_priority()` now treats `planned`, `in_progress`, `blocked`, and `cancelled` as the same priority, so `latest_track_dir()` will pick whichever active track has the newest `updated_at`. That no longer matches `render_registry()`'s planned-last ordering (`libs/infrastructure/src/track/render.rs:469-470`), so a newer planned track can become the verifier's "latest track" while the registry still surfaces an older in-progress track. In a mixed repo, `verify()` can therefore skip artifact validation for the wrong track.

This test no longer exercises the ordering it is meant to protect: with only a single snapshot, it will pass even if `render_registry` stops putting planned tracks after in-progress/blocked/cancelled tracks. The deleted multi-track assertions were the only coverage for the Current Focus sort.

`latest_track_dir` still breaks same-timestamp ties in the opposite direction from `render_registry`: snapshots are sorted by `track.id()` ascending before rendering (`render.rs:342-345`), but the verification path ranks equal `(priority, updated_at)` entries by `dir_name` and picks the lexicographically larger one (`latest_track.rs:150-153`). If two active tracks share an `updated_at`, `registry.md` and verification can disagree on which track is latest.

The public `PreCommitTypeSignalsService` contract still documents fields/errors that this change removed: it says the output contains optional layer bindings, and the `run` errors mention metadata failures even though `PreCommitTypeSignalsOutput` now only carries blocked/red/yellow signals and the metadata/impl-plan variants were replaced by branch errors. This leaves a contradictory usecase API contract for callers implementing the new branch-guard flow.

After removing the `plan/` short-circuit, this gate still treats any non-`track/` branch as a valid track identifier by falling back to the full branch name. A PR from `plan/foo` can therefore reach the strict merge gate instead of being rejected as outside the track workflow, and can pass if the expected blobs exist. For the lane expunge, non-`track/` refs should fail closed here rather than being evaluated as best-effort track IDs.

`plan/` branches now fall through this function because the old short-circuit was removed, but the track id derivation still accepts non-`track/` branches as `track_id_str = branch`. That leaves `plan/foo` eligible to pass the task-completion guard if an impl-plan with resolved tasks is present, instead of failing closed as a non-track PR head. Reject non-`track/` branches before reading impl-plan.json.
